name: Blue Dot Cab Co
districts_or_customers: general public in Madisonville/Paducah tristate area
recent_news: 2017 expansion into Paducah market per Paducah Sun coverage; brand identity is taxi/rail-crew, not school transport.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in fit. This is a taxi / rail-crew transport / courier business — no school-bus or SPED contracts indicated. Outside Summit's student-transportation scope.

name: Sendaride Inc
owner_or_parent: Venture-backed (independent corporate, VC investors including healthcare/tech angels; not a school-bus operator)
recent_news: Continues to expand NEMT concierge platform nationally; added healthcare advisory board members and partnership with EMBARK seniors program.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in. SendaRide is a tech-enabled NEMT platform (Uber-like model with vetted drivers), not a school-bus operator with its own fleet. Out of scope for Summit.

name: Sioux Falls Wheelchair Transit
districts_or_customers: individual private-pay riders and Medicaid in Sioux Falls metro
cross-border service into MN, IA, NE
recent_news: No M&A news. Listed on LifeScape, 211 HelpLine Center, and Minnehaha County specialized transport directories as active provider.
fit_notes: Not a school-bus / SPED contractor — operates wheelchair/medical (NEMT) van service for adults and elderly. Out of scope for Summit's student-transportation tuck-in thesis.

name: Kyle B Flook School Bus LLC
districts_or_customers: likely Gettysburg Area School District or Upper Adams SD area (Goldenville Rd, Adams County PA)
recent_news: No public M&A or news coverage. Owner is a private individual (~40s), 955 Goldenville Rd Gettysburg PA; no company website found.
fit_notes: Very small (est. ~3-7 buses) independent PA contractor in Summit's expansion state. Likely too small to be a stand-alone tuck-in but could be a roll-in if Summit already operates in Adams County / South-Central PA. Worth a passive note in the PA cluster build, not a priority outreach.

name: Spalding Bus Line
districts_or_customers: likely Monroe City R-1 School District (Monroe City, MO) and possibly neighboring Northeast Missouri districts
recent_news: No M&A news. Long-standing local family operation since 1999; revenue est. ~$230K-$310K with ~7-9 employees.
fit_notes: Very small (~6-9 buses) family operator in NE Missouri, 25+ years under one owner — potential succession candidate if owner is approaching retirement. Too small as a stand-alone, but could be a low-cost tuck-in if Summit builds an NE-MO / Hannibal-area cluster. Outside Summit's current operating states except via expansion.
